intro: Eriosoriopsis rosthorniana, also known as the Himalayan Sweet Flag, is a perennial herb belonging to the Zingiberaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Eriosoriopsis rosthorniana is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of traditional Ayurvedic formulations such as churnas and powders.
